div.head: The Consul General at Shanghai (Cabot) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 359. Following news appears Shanghai press 2nd on Li Tsung-jen’s unofficial peace delegation local nonpartisan leaders appointed 31st (ReContel to Nanking 259, February 1, to Department 340).

Chang Shih-chaoMember of the National Policy Advisory Committee. on 1st denied reports circulating city that mission of delegation will be seek separate peace for Shanghai which he described as premature in view absence immediate military threat. Chang stated delegation would visit Peiping shortly under instructions
“to observe conditions, talk with officials there and report to President,” and that it was not empowered to make decisions or to act in capacity similar to official peace delegations.
Secret meeting of delegates presided over by W. W. YenChinese Premier at Peking in 1920’s. leads press to speculate whether all delegates agreeable accept mission.
